summary.intent: Fix PR review comments on a Rust html-to-text conversion feature, then resolve all review conversations on the GitHub PR.
summary.outcome: All 4 original review comments were addressed (warn logging, safe width constant, TrivialDecorator for clean text, test added), then 2 additional Greptile comments were fixed (U+FFFD assertion in test, improved warn message for raw HTML fallback). All conversations were replied to and marked resolved.

repo: Project uses tracing::warn! for non-fatal errors rather than silent failures
html2text crate: TrivialDecorator strips all link/markup annotations (cleanest output), PlainDecorator wraps links in [...], include_link_footnotes defaults to false
usize::MAX is unsafe for width on 32-bit targets; 1_000_000 is used as a safe 'no wrapping' constant
PR reviews from Greptile bot follow up with additional comments after initial fixes are pushed

code.finding: html_to_text fallback on conversion error: logs with tracing::warn! and returns raw HTML body (with message clarifying it's raw HTML), instead of empty string
Uses TrivialDecorator (not PlainDecorator) to avoid link bracket annotations in converted text output

workflow: GitHub PR review comments require POST to /pulls/comments/{id}/replies endpoint, not a general comments endpoint
Resolving conversations requires separate API calls after pushing fixes — the assistant initially forgot this step
Running cargo fmt check and tests before committing is part of the standard workflow

friction: Initial test run failed due to private module path compilation error, requiring an edit before tests passed
GitHub comment resolution was forgotten after the first round of fixes and required a follow-up user prompt
Needed multiple Bash calls to explore html2text API (TrivialDecorator, PlainDecorator, include_link_footnotes behavior) before understanding the right approach
Reply endpoint discovery required trial and error — first attempt used wrong HTTP method
open_items: Greptile suggested ideally stripping HTML tags in the fallback path rather than returning raw HTML — this was only partially addressed (warn message updated, but tag stripping was not implemented)
